one_line_takeaway: Finsta is a plug-and-play scene-graph-based post-training module that distills fine-grained spatial (object) and temporal (predicate/motion) alignment into existing video-language models, consistently lifting 13 backbones across 12 datasets while adding zero inference-time cost.
key_finding: Finsta consistently improves all 13 backbone VLMs across all 6 tasks and sets new state-of-the-art on multiple benchmarks in both fine-tuning and zero-shot settings, with gains largest for backbones that have the full dual-stream-sum (text + video + multimodal) architecture and for long-form video (where the long-form-trained LFVILA L-Vid variant gains dramatically). Zero-shot gains exceed fine-tuning gains (e.g., Finsta-InternVideo +15.6 Top-1 on K400 zero-shot), and ablations show the two contrastive alignment losses are the most load-bearing components (removing PTC costs -7.9% average; removing OSC -5.9%; removing STGD-GTrm -5.1%).
core_contribution: The paper proposes Finsta (Fine-grained structural spatio-temporal alignment), a plug-and-play post-training module that enhances existing video-language models (VLMs) by aligning them at the level of scene-graph (SG) structure rather than coarse whole-video/whole-text embeddings. Its own novel components are: (1) a scene-graph formulation that unifies a retrofitted Textual SG (TSG, augmented with adverbial-modifier attribute nodes for predicates), a video Dynamic SG (DSG, whose per-keyframe graphs are stitched via IoU-based temporal coreference edges = object tracking), and a Holistic SG (HSG) that links TSG and DSG through CLIP-based cross-modal coreference edges; (2) a Recurrent Graph Transformer (R-GTrm) that adds a gated temporal-fusion of prior-frame queries to a standard graph Transformer (GTrm, which is cited prior work) to model DSG/HSG dynamics; (3) a Spatial-Temporal Gaussian Differential Graph Transformer (STGD-GTrm) that uses a Gaussian kernel over bounding-box centroid displacement to distinguish moving vs. stationary objects; (4) two unsupervised contrastive objectives, high-order Object-centered Spatial Contrasting (OSC) and high-order Predicate-centered Temporal Contrasting (PTC); and (5) a knowledge-distillation 'representation transfer' that warm-starts Finsta from a host VLM's first-layer features and distills Finsta's aligned [CLS] features back into the host, so that SG parsing is needed ONLY during post-training and the host VLM runs alone at inference. Note: the graph Transformer (GTrm [22]), scene-graph representations, textual/dynamic SG parsers, and CLIP are cited building blocks, not this paper's contribution; the paper's own novelty is the R-GTrm, STGD-GTrm, OSC/PTC alignment, and the whole plug-and-play SG-distillation pipeline.
threat_model: Not applicable. This is a representation-learning / multimodal-understanding paper with no adversary, no attack surface, and no security or privacy claim. No threat model is present in the paper and none should be imposed.
limitations: (1) Efficacy depends on the host VLM having the dual-stream-sum architecture; backbones missing encoders gain the least (All-in-one, VideoCLIP, CLIP4Clip consistently show the smallest improvements because distillation targets for absent encoders are dropped). (2) The pipeline depends heavily on SG parsing quality (FasterRCNN/MOTIFS/attribute classifiers, dependency parser), which the authors themselves note introduces noise; the plug-and-play distillation design is explicitly motivated as a way to keep this parsing noise out of inference. (3) SG annotations and extra post-training compute (16 A100s) are still required during the post-training stage. (4) Anomalously large jumps (e.g., ActivityNet Video-Paragraph Retrieval R@1 +34.5 for Finsta-LFVILA L-Vid) are largely attributable to training the backbone on long-form data rather than Finsta alone, which the paper acknowledges. (5) No security, robustness, or adversarial evaluation (out of scope for the paper's goals).

datasets_used: Kinetics-400 (K400) and Something-Something-V2 (SSV2) for Video Action Recognition
YouCook2 and MSR-VTT for Video Captioning
LSMDC and DiDeMo for Video-Text Retrieval
MSR-VTT-QA, MSVD-QA, TGIF-Frame for Video Question Answering
QuerYD and ActivityNet for Video-Paragraph Retrieval
How2QA and VIOLIN for Long-Form Video Question Answering
Post-training corpora: 25K from WebVid-2.5M + 25K from HD-VILA-100M (50K short-form pairs); 30K from LF-VILA-8M for long-form (avg duration 100.2s, avg text 307.9 tokens)
TSG vocabulary after >5-occurrence filtering: 7,021 objects, 2,256 relations, 4,895 attributes
quantitative_results: Video Action Recognition (Top-1): Finsta-InternVideo 93.7 on K400 (+2.6) and 80.5 on SSV2 (+3.3), both new SOTA; Finsta-HDVILA 83.4 on K400 (+4.8)
Video Captioning: Finsta-HDVILA YouCook2 METEOR 18.8 (+5.3), BLEU@4 12.7 (+4.5); Finsta-Clover MSR-VTT METEOR 38.8 (+4.7)
Video-Text Retrieval: Finsta-HDVILA DiDeMo R@1 41.3 (+12.5), R@5 70.9 (+13.5); HDVILA averages +10.3% recall; Finsta-Clover DiDeMo R@1 56.0 (+5.9)
Video QA (Acc.): Finsta-Video-LLaVA MSVD-QA 72.5 (+6.8), MSR-VTT-MC 99.4 (+2.6); Finsta-VIOLET MSVD-QA 54.6 (+6.7)
Video-Paragraph Retrieval: Finsta-LFVILA (L-Vid) ActivityNet R@1 69.8 (+34.5), R@5 92.5 (+27.1), QuerYD R@1 78.6 (+8.9), new SOTA
Long-Form Video QA (Acc.): Finsta-LFVILA (L-Vid) How2QA 84.8 (+8.7), VIOLIN 78.0 (+7.1)
Zero-shot: Finsta-InternVideo K400 Top-1 68.9 (+15.6), LSMDC R@1 21.9 (+10.9), MSVD-QA 29.8 (+16.3); Finsta-Video-LLaVA MSVD-QA zero-shot 45.3 (+20.2)

Ablation (avg over 6 datasets, drop vs full 62.9): w/o PTC loss -7.9, w/o OSC loss -5.9, w/o STGD-GTrm -5.1, R-GTrm->RGNN -3.9, w/o temporal coref edge -3.7, w/o high-order (PTC) -5.1, w/o adverbial modifier -2.9, GTrm->GAT -2.9, w/o cross-modal coref edge -2.4
baselines_compared: Backbone VLMs enhanced by Finsta (10): UniVL, HDVILA, Clover, LFVILA, VIOLET, VideoCLIP, CLIP4Clip, MCQ, All-in-one, InternVideo
Backbone large video LLMs enhanced by Finsta (3): Video-LLaMA, Video-ChatGPT, Video-LLaVA

External comparison systems: TimeSformer, Frozen, OmniVL, ViViT, Swin, UniFormer-V2, VideoMAE (action recognition); VideoBERT, IcoCap, HiTeA, VLAB (captioning); OA-Trans, ALPRO, CAMoE, UMT-L (retrieval); ClipBERT, HiTeA, VLAB (VQA); TeachText, TESTA (paragraph retrieval); ResNet-SF, GVE, HERO (long-form QA); ImageBind, LanguageBind (zero-shot); DRN, VSLNet (language video localization)
Encoder-alternative ablations: GAT in place of GTrm, RGNN in place of R-GTrm
